customer.primaryUseCase: AI support agent for internal claims operations
customer.businessProblem: Claims operations staff repeatedly ask the same policy interpretation and process questions, creating a high volume of low-value queries for the policy and training teams. Staff have low confidence in answers from colleagues. New joiners take 6-8 weeks to reach productivity.
customer.desiredOutcome: Reduce repetitive internal queries by 50%. Improve answer accuracy and consistency. Reduce new joiner ramp time to 4 weeks. Maintain source citations for all answers.

discovery.currentProcess: Claims staff ask questions via internal Slack channels or email to policy and training teams. Answers are given ad hoc. No single source of truth. New joiners shadow colleagues and attend a 3-day induction. Policy documents are in SharePoint but difficult to navigate.
discovery.usersAffected: 85 claims handlers, 12 claims managers, 8 policy specialists, 4 trainers
discovery.constraints: Data must stay within the organisation — no external processing of policy content. Agent must cite sources for all answers. Escalation to human expert must be seamless. FCA compliance — advice boundary must be clear (internal process only, not customer-facing). Staff have low technical appetite.
discovery.knownRisks: Outdated knowledge base content. Permission issues — not all staff can access all policy areas. Inaccurate answers reducing trust. Poorly designed escalation workflow. Low staff adoption. AI giving confidently wrong answers.
discovery.successDefinition: 50% reduction in repetitive Slack queries to policy team. Answer accuracy above 90% on tested queries. Source citation in 100% of answers. New joiner time-to-productivity under 4 weeks.
discovery.buyerTeam: Chief Operating Officer, Head of Claims, IT Director

stakeholders.name: Tom Bradley
requiredActions: Define priority policy areas for knowledge base

stakeholders.name: Claire Hutchings
requiredActions: Review and validate knowledge base content

workflows.name: Staff Question Submission
workflows.description: Claims handler asks a question via Slack #claims-questions channel or direct message to policy specialist.
painPoints: Answers not searchable after the fact

workflows.name: Policy Document Lookup
workflows.description: Staff searches SharePoint for the relevant policy document and navigates to the relevant section.
painPoints: Documents are long and hard to navigate
Staff don't know which document to look in
failureModes: Staff give up and ask a colleague

workflows.name: Escalation to Expert
workflows.description: When staff cannot find an answer or are unsure, they escalate to a policy specialist or senior claims manager.
painPoints: Context lost when moving from Slack to email
failureModes: Context not captured for future reference

systems.name: SharePoint (Policy Documents)
systems.notes: Microsoft 365 SharePoint. Graph API available. Document library structure is inconsistent. Version control is manual. Content ownership is unclear.

systems.name: Slack
systems.notes: Standard Slack API. Bot integration straightforward. Need to define which channels the agent operates in.

systems.name: Claims Management System
systems.notes: Legacy Guidewire implementation. IT to confirm API availability. Likely out of scope for MVP — agent focuses on policy Q&A, not claims data.

systems.name: Internal Ticketing System
systems.notes: ServiceNow. Used for IT helpdesk. Could be used for escalation routing from AI agent to human expert.

dataSources.name: Policy Document Library
openQuestions: Which documents are current vs archived?

dataSources.name: Slack Q&A History
openQuestions: Are there private channels with relevant Q&A?

dataSources.name: Training Materials
openQuestions: Are training materials aligned with current policy?

risks.title: Outdated policy documents in knowledge base
risks.description: SharePoint contains multiple versions of policy documents. AI may retrieve and cite outdated guidance, causing operational errors.
risks.mitigation: Document audit before ingestion. Mark current vs archived. Assign content owners per policy area. Implement update notification process.
risks.escalationTrigger: Any answer citing a superseded policy document

risks.title: AI giving confidently wrong answers
risks.description: Agent may answer out-of-scope or ambiguous questions with false confidence, undermining trust.
risks.mitigation: Calibrate confidence thresholds. Display uncertainty when confidence is low. Route low-confidence answers to escalation. Regular accuracy testing against known questions.
risks.escalationTrigger: Answer accuracy below 85% in any weekly test batch

risks.title: Permission issues — staff accessing policy areas they cannot use
risks.description: Not all claims handlers should access all policy areas. AI agent may surface restricted content.
risks.mitigation: Implement role-based knowledge base segmentation. Map Slack/SSO roles to policy access levels. IT to confirm SharePoint permission model.
risks.escalationTrigger: Any claim handler receiving content outside their access level

risks.title: Low staff adoption
risks.description: Claims handlers may default to asking colleagues rather than using the AI agent, reducing ROI.
risks.mitigation: Seamless Slack integration — zero friction to use. Manager communications on adoption. Track usage rate weekly. Gather and respond to user feedback during pilot.
risks.escalationTrigger: Weekly active users below 30% of pilot group after 2 weeks

risks.title: FCA advice boundary — agent perceived as regulatory guidance
risks.description: If staff perceive agent answers as FCA-regulated advice rather than internal process guidance, it creates regulatory risk.
risks.mitigation: All agent answers include disclaimer: 'This is internal process guidance only. For regulatory queries, contact the Policy team.' FCA compliance review before launch.
risks.escalationTrigger: Any complaint or regulatory query about AI guidance

pilotPlan.objective: Validate AI support agent reduces repetitive policy queries and improves answer consistency for claims handlers.
pilotPlan.scope: Motor claims policy area only. Slack integration only (no email). 15 pilot users from claims handling team.
pilotUsers: 15 claims handlers from motor claims team (TBC with Tom Bradley)

successMetrics.name: Repetitive query volume reduction
successMetrics.measurementMethod: Slack message count in #claims-questions from pilot users

successMetrics.name: Answer accuracy
successMetrics.measurementMethod: Weekly batch of 20 test questions reviewed by Claire Hutchings

successMetrics.name: Source citation rate
successMetrics.target: 100% of answers include source document and section
successMetrics.measurementMethod: Automated check on all agent responses

successMetrics.name: Staff satisfaction score
successMetrics.measurementMethod: Thumbs up/down rating on each agent response in Slack

launchCriteria: Policy document audit complete for motor claims area
Slack app deployed and tested in staging
Role-based access controls configured and tested
rollbackCriteria: Answer accuracy below 80% in any weekly test batch
Any staff receiving content outside their access level
Agent downtime above 4 hours in any working day
pilotPlan.baselineMeasurement: 6 weeks of Slack message volume in #claims-questions. Baseline accuracy test on 50 known motor policy questions.
pilotPlan.targetOutcome: 50% reduction in repetitive queries. 90% answer accuracy. 100% source citation. Staff satisfaction 3.5/5.
